methods.method_name: XRF Analysis for Sapropel Layer Identification
methods.description: This method involves using XRF analysis to identify and characterize Sapropel layers in sediment cores based on lightness values and elemental ratios. It is used to precisely identify Sapropel units and their interruptions.

steps.description: Obtain high-resolution images of the sediments using the Avaatech XRF core scanner.
steps.searchable_summary: capture high-resolution sediment images with XRF core scanner

steps.description: Use lightness values (L*) from the images to identify Sapropel units and interruptions.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze lightness values to identify Sapropel layers

steps.description: Analyze XRF elemental ratios such as Ba/Al and S/Al to observe changes through S1a and S1b intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze XRF elemental ratios for interval changes

methods.description: This method constructs an age model for sediment core ND14Mbis using radiocarbon AMS measurements and Bayesian statistics. It is used to estimate sedimentation rates and infer sub-millennial events.

steps.description: Collect 3-6 mg per sample of monospecific G. ruber white variety (and G. bulloides in some cases) from the >150 µm fraction.
steps.searchable_summary: collect foraminifera samples for radiocarbon dating

steps.description: Graphitize samples at the Godwin Laboratory for Palaeoclimate Research.
Analyze samples at the 14 CHRONO Centre of the Queen's University of Belfast.
Calibrate radiocarbon ages using MARINE13 calibration curves to obtain calendar ages.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate radiocarbon ages to calendar ages

steps.description: Construct the age model using Bayesian statistics software Bacon with R.
steps.searchable_summary: construct age model using Bayesian statistics

steps.description: Estimate sedimentation rates based on the constructed age model.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate sedimentation rates from age model
